content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Ms. Shirley F. Knopf 
Attorney at Law 
March 4, 1987 
suite 818 The Professional Building 
117 East Eighth street 
Long Beach, CA 
90813 
Re: 
Your Request for Assis~ance; 
Our File No. I-87-072 
Dear Ms. Knopf: 
Your letter of February 18, 1987, was forwarded to this 
office by the Secretary of State. 
Your question involves the 
interpretation of Elections Code Section 12404. 
That provision 
is not part of the Political Reform Act. 
(Government Code 
sections 81000-91015.) 
This agency does not interpret the 
Elections Code provision. 
However, as I advised your office by 
telephone on March 3, 1987, you may direct your inquiry to the 
Attorney General's Office for a response. 
Sincerely, 
--

Dear Sir or Madam: 
Bills have been 
sented to the Treasurer of Jim Wilson 
deceased's campa gn funds for medical bills, hospital bills 
and expenses relating to funeral 
s.

Wilson's 
ampa 
fund 
requests a ruling as to whether these are 
expenses to 
be paid under Section 12 
of the Election Code. 
Thank you very much. 
Very

sections.conclusion: That provision is not part of the Political Reform Act. (Government Code sections 81000-91015.) This agency does not interpret the Elections Code provision. However, as I advised your office by telephone on March 3, 1987, you may direct your inquiry to the Attorney General's Office for a response.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether medical bills, hospital bills, and funeral expenses for a deceased campaign candidate may be paid from campaign funds under Elections Code Section 12404.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declines to provide an interpretation because Elections Code Section 12404 is not part of the Political Reform Act, which is outside the FPPC's jurisdiction. The requester is directed to the Attorney General's Office for guidance on this Elections Code question.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both incoming request (February 18 letter from Knopf) and FPPC response (March 4 letter from Leidigh). Marked as advice_letter because primary document is FPPC response. No traditional QUESTION or CONCLUSION section headers present; question and conclusion extracted from letter text. This is a declination/referral letter rather than substantive advice.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether medical bills, hospital bills, and funeral expenses for a deceased campaign candidate may be paid from campaign funds under Elections Code Section 12404.

CONCLUSION: That provision is not part of the Political Reform Act. (Government Code sections 81000-91015.) This agency does not interpret the Elections Code provision. However, as I advised your office by telephone on March 3, 1987, you may direct your inquiry to the Attorney General's Office for a response.

embedding.summary: Ms. Knopf requested FPPC guidance on whether medical, hospital, and funeral expenses for a deceased campaign candidate could be paid from campaign funds under Elections Code Section 12404. The FPPC declined jurisdiction, explaining that Elections Code provisions are outside its authority and referring the inquiry to the Attorney General's Office.
